task_completion.visual_evidence: The NextGen Connect (Mirth Connect) Administrator GUI shows a channel named 'Lab_Order_Sender' in the Channels list.

task_completion.description: Configure the source as a TCP Listener on port 6661
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Source tab for the channel shows the connector type as 'TCP Listener' and the port set to '6661'.

task_completion.description: Configure the destination as a TCP Sender to localhost:6699
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Destinations tab shows the connector type as 'TCP Sender' with the remote address set to 'localhost' (or '127.0.0.1') and the port set to '6699'.

task_completion.description: Configure NACK handling to set message status to ERROR for 'AE' or 'AR' codes
task_completion.visual_evidence: A script or configuration in the Response Transformer or Destination shows logic checking the MSA-1 field for 'AE' or 'AR' and setting the message status to ERROR (e.g., using responseMap, responseStatus, or similar Mirth API methods).

task_completion.description: Configure logging to append MSA-3 error text to /home/ga/lab_rejections.log
task_completion.visual_evidence: A script (e.g., JavaScript writer or File Writer destination/step) shows logic extracting the MSA-3 field and appending it to the file path '/home/ga/lab_rejections.log'.

task_completion.description: Deploy the channel and process a message
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NextGen Connect dashboard shows the channel as 'Deployed' and 'Started', OR a terminal window shows the contents of '/home/ga/lab_rejections.log' containing the expected error text.

integrity.description: Agent used the NextGen Connect Administrator GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the actual NextGen Connect Administrator Java application, rather than just writing XML configuration files in a text editor.

integrity.description: Agent dynamically extracted the error text instead of hardcoding it
integrity.visual_evidence: The script extracts the error text from the HL7 message structure (e.g., msg['MSA']['MSA.3'].toString()) rather than hardcoding the string 'Simulated Patient ID Error' into the log writer.

integrity.description: Log file was generated by the channel, not manually faked
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal history does not show the agent manually writing the expected error text directly to the log file using commands like 'echo "Simulated Patient ID Error" > /home/ga/lab_rejections.log'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The channel name must be 'Lab_Order_Sender'. The source port is 6661 and the destination port is 6699. The log file must be written to '/home/ga/lab_rejections.log'. The sample HL7 file is located at '/home/ga/sample_orm.hl7'. When successfully configured and tested against the simulated LIS server, the expected error text extracted from MSA-3 and written to the log file will be 'Simulated Patient ID Error'.
